records.ety: [L. alpha, Gr. 'a`lfa, from Heb. āleph, name of the first letter in the alphabet, also meaning ox.]

defs: The first letter in the Greek alphabet, answering to A, and hence used to denote the beginning.
quotes: In am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end, the first and the last.
notes: Formerly used also denote the chief; as, Plato was the alpha of the wits.
☞ In cataloguing stars, the brightest star of a constellation in designated by Alpha (); as, Lyræ.
